macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Stocks Close Mixed Amid Iran War Tensions and Trump Statements
items.summary: U.S. stocks ended mixed after President Trump vowed to hit Iran 'extremely hard' in coming weeks, while oil prices soared over 10% on Thursday. Separately, the Dow surged 1,000 points earlier as Trump signaled a possible off-ramp from escalation.[2]

items.title: March Jobs Report Shows Solid Gains Despite Prior Revision
items.summary: The Labor Department reported stronger-than-expected job growth in March, exceeding economist estimates of 40,000 with upward revision to February's figures. Wages grew but at a slower pace than recent years.[1][3]

items.title: Gas Prices Surge Above $4/Gallon Due to Iran Conflict Oil Shock
items.summary: National average gas prices topped $4 per gallon for the first time since 2022, driven by Iran war disruptions pushing oil over $100/barrel and a 34% monthly rise. Prices expected to continue climbing.[1][2]

items.title: Fed Officials Warn of Inflation Risks from Energy Price Spike
items.summary: Fed President John Williams cautioned that rising energy prices from the Iran War could spread inflation across sectors; Chair Powell highlighted a new supply shock as oil tops $100/barrel. Goldman Sachs raised its inflation forecast.[1]

items.title: 30-Year Mortgage Rates Climb to 6.46% Amid Economic Pressures
items.summary: The average 30-year fixed mortgage rate rose to 6.46% this week from 6.38%, marking the highest since September per Freddie Mac data. Earlier reports noted a jump from 6.22% to 6.38%.[1][2]

items.title: Amazon Imposes 3.5% Fuel Surcharge on Third-Party Sellers
items.summary: Amazon will add a 3.5% surcharge on third-party sellers starting later this month, citing war-driven fuel price spikes and logistics costs.[2]

items.title: Oracle Lays Off Thousands in Shift to AI Investments
items.summary: Oracle reportedly laid off thousands of workers as part of reallocating resources toward AI investments.[2]

items.title: Tesla Sales Rise but Fall Short After Musk Boycotts
items.summary: Tesla reported rising sales following a tough year of boycotts related to Elon Musk, though results still missed expectations.[2]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: ONG Leads Crypto Gainers with 38.39% Surge to $0.0911
items.summary: Ontology Gas (ONG) topped 24-hour gainers with a 38.39% price increase to $0.0911, backed by $32.86 million in trading volume. YB followed with 28.71% gains to $0.15 and $59.66 million volume.

items.title: BLUR Surges 40.30% in Bitcoin World Top Gainers
items.summary: BLUR led Bitcoin world gainers with a 40.30% rise to $0.0243 and $99.24 million volume, followed by NOM up 27.58% to $0.0057 with $136.58 million volume. ONT gained 25.30% to $0.1042.

items.title: NTRN Explodes 134.78% as Top Altcoin Gainer
items.summary: Neutron (NTRN) surged 134.78% to $0.0054 with $5.12 million volume, NOM rose 72.38% to $0.0101 with over $349 million volume, and STO gained 62.45% to $0.7286.

items.title: Algorand (ALGO) Soars 15% on Post-Quantum Cryptography Recognition
items.summary: ALGO price rose 15.38% to $0.1185 with trading volume up 62.69% to $199.7 million, boosted by Google's citation of its post-quantum cryptography features.

items.title: Ethereum Drops Nearly 5% Amid $94M Liquidations
items.summary: Ethereum fell nearly 5% to around $2,000 after climbing above $2,150, triggered by high leverage and Open Interest leading to $94 million in liquidations, the highest in the sector.

items.title: Bitcoin Dips 2.5% to $66,938 as Bloomberg Warns of Potential Crash
items.summary: Bitcoin traded down 2.5% to $66,938 amid market volatility, with a Bloomberg strategist forecasting a possible return to $10,000 due to heightened risks.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Microsoft Launches Three New In-House AI Models Targeting OpenAI and Google
items.summary: Microsoft released three foundational AI models built entirely in-house, including a state-of-the-art speech transcription system, positioning it as a direct competitor to OpenAI and Google.

items.title: Arcee Releases Open-Source Trinity-Large-Thinking Model for Enterprises
items.summary: Arcee unveiled Trinity-Large-Thinking, a powerful U.S.-made open-source AI model that enterprises can download and customize, continuing the trend of accessible AI tools.

items.title: Microsoft Announces $10 Billion Investment in Japan AI Data Centers
items.summary: Microsoft plans to invest $10 billion over four years in Japan to build AI data centers, boosting local infrastructure and partnerships like with Sakura Internet.

items.title: Meta Pauses Work with Mercor After Major Data Breach
items.summary: Meta halted collaboration with AI data vendor Mercor following a security incident that potentially exposed industry secrets, prompting investigations by major AI labs.

items.title: OpenAI Acquires Tech Talk Show TBPN in First Media Purchase
items.summary: OpenAI bought Silicon Valley's TBPN, a popular tech-focused talk show, to drive AI conversations, rooted in long-standing ties with its founders.

items.title: Mercor Confirms Data Breach Affecting OpenAI and Anthropic Partners
items.summary: AI startup Mercor, which supplies data to OpenAI and Anthropic, confirmed a data breach that has raised security concerns across the industry.

items.title: Sony Acquires UK-Based Machine Learning Company
items.summary: Sony expanded its AI capabilities by acquiring a UK machine learning firm, signaling continued consolidation in the sector.

items.title: Take-Two Lays Off AI Team Including Head of Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: Take-Two Interactive reportedly laid off part of its AI team, including its Head of AI, amid industry realignments.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Marathon Extraction Shooter Reveals Release Date, Pricing, Voice Cast, and System Requirements
items.summary: Bungie's Marathon launches on March 5 for $40 with a stacked voice cast and a limited edition DualSense controller revealed. Recommended system requirements have also been announced.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass Confirms June 2025 Wave 1 Lineup Ahead of Xbox Games Showcase
items.summary: Microsoft has unveiled the full list of games joining Xbox Game Pass in the first wave of June 2025.

items.title: Nioh 3 Announced at Sony’s State of Play, Available to Play Immediately
items.summary: Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo announce Nioh 3 during Sony’s June State of Play showcase, allowing soulslike fans to dive in right away.

items.title: No More Heroes Developer Reveals New Ultraviolent Sci-Fi Game with Dead Rising Vibes
items.summary: Suda51 and Grasshopper Manufacture unveil their latest over-the-top action game combining elements of No More Heroes and Dead Rising.

items.title: Helldivers 2 Announces ‘Force of Law’ Warbond
items.summary: Helldivers 2 reveals a new Force of Law Warbond coming soon to the cooperative shooter.

items.title: Bloodstained Sequel The Scarlet Engagement Revealed at State of Play
items.summary: Developer ArtPlay unveils The Scarlet Engagement, a new entry in the Bloodstained series that looks nothing like the original.

items.title: Uncharted 5 Speculation Surges After Naughty Dog Director's Cryptic Post
items.summary: Hopes for Uncharted 5 intensify following an Instagram post from Naughty Dog's Shaun Escayg hinting at research resembling an Uncharted setting.

items.title: Bubsy 4D Announced as Franchise Revival After 30 Years
items.summary: Fabraz reveals Bubsy 4D, a high-speed 3D platformer with improved gameplay and eye-catching graphics for retro fans.

items.title: Star Fox Game Rumored for Summer Release
items.summary: Leaker Nate the Hate mentions a new Star Fox game coming this summer on a podcast.

items.title: WoW Lead Producer Leaves Blizzard for Riot's MMO
items.summary: World of Warcraft's lead producer departs Blizzard to join Riot Games' long-in-development MMO project.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Sony Interactive Entertainment Acquires Cinemersive Labs
items.summary: Sony has acquired UK-based startup Cinemersive Labs, which develops tools to convert 2D photos and videos into 3D volumetric photographs. The startup will join Sony's Visual Computing Group.

items.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Emergency Windows 11 Update
items.summary: Microsoft released an emergency update for Windows 11 to fix issues from a broken March preview patch. Separately, Microsoft is forcibly installing Windows 11 25H2 on more computers.

items.title: Samsung Rolls Out Google Cast Support to TVs
items.summary: Samsung is adding Google Cast support to its TV lineups for free, with the rollout now underway. Some Samsung TVs are already receiving the update.

items.title: Google Pixel 11 Pro XL Leaked in CAD Renders
items.summary: New CAD-based renders of the Google Pixel 11 Pro XL have leaked, completing the family and showcasing a bold new camera bar design.

items.title: Oppo Find X9 Ultra Appears in Real-Life Photos
items.summary: The Oppo Find X9 Ultra has been shown in real-life images, reportedly overtaking the Vivo X300 Ultra in certain aspects.

items.title: Iran Claims Strikes on Oracle and Amazon Data Centers
items.summary: Iran's IRGC claimed attacks on an Oracle data center in Dubai and an Amazon cloud facility in Bahrain, threatening major US tech companies. Dubai authorities denied the Oracle strike.

items.title: North Korean Hackers Steal $280 Million from Drift
items.summary: North Korean hackers seized $280 million from cryptocurrency firm Drift. Separately, UNC1069 conducted a social engineering attack on an Axios maintainer leading to an npm supply chain compromise.
